title: Root Nodules and Nitrogen: Nature's Secret
script_summary: Discover how root nodules play a vital role in nitrogen fixation for plants.

segments.voiceover: What if I told you that plants have a hidden superpower?
Root nodules are tiny structures on plant roots, home to bacteria that transform nitrogen from the air into a form plants can use.
Without these nodules, plants would struggle to grow, especially in nutrient-poor soils.
Studies reveal that legumes, like peas and beans, host these nodules, making them vital for agriculture.
So, the next time you eat legumes, remember, you’re tapping into nature’s nitrogen factory!
Want more science secrets? Hit that follow button and explore the wonders of nature!
